purpose: Measure whether SPY historical return accrued overnight or intraday after fixed daily trading costs; this is not a candidate decision.

accounts.B1_SPY_BUY_HOLD: Buy whole shares at the first raw open, mark with the adjusted close ratio, and value every day after hypothetical liquidation cost.
accounts.B2_SPY_OVERNIGHT: Each accepted session close buy whole shares; sell at the next accepted session open using adj_open_t / adj_close_t_minus_1; remain cash intraday.
accounts.B3_SPY_INTRADAY: Each accepted session open buy whole shares; sell at the same accepted session close using adj_close_t / adj_open_t; remain cash overnight.

execution.split_boundary: Every account starts from USD 40000 inside each individually reported split. Combined validation plus holdout chains the two independently simulated account-return paths through cash, with no B1 holding period or B2 overnight interval crossing the boundary.
execution.corporate_action_semantics: Use provider-adjusted endpoint ratios solely to translate raw entry notional into a corporate-action-complete exit proxy.

costs.B1: one entry and one hypothetical liquidation per isolated segment; combined validation plus holdout therefore has two entries and two hypothetical liquidations
costs.B2_B3: one entry and one exit for every complete interval

one_use.before_claim: Verify clean pushed code identity, exact definition bytes, database file identity, schema, date-key metadata, split counts, and absence of any WAL without reading price rows.
one_use.after_durable_claim: Read price rows, compute account outcomes, adjudicate gates, and publish canonical result and receipt bytes. Every result binds the exact canonical claim SHA-256, and result bytes are recaptured immediately before receipt finalization.
one_use.preflight_failure: No result or receipt is created and the first outcome run remains unconsumed.
one_use.interruption_after_claim: The run remains consumed. Recovery verifies the exact code, claim, database arguments and metadata, then publishes EXECUTION_ERROR_CONSUMED without reading price rows or rerunning outcomes.

metric_formulas.maximum_positive_year_contribution: For each calendar year, compute end-of-year NAV minus start-of-year NAV in USD on the continuous account path. Divide the largest strictly positive annual USD P&L by the sum of all strictly positive annual USD P&L. If no year has positive USD P&L, publish null and fail the concentration gate.
metric_formulas.maximum_positive_observation_contributio: Largest strictly positive observation return divided by the sum of strictly positive observation returns; null when none are positive. This is descriptive and not a gate.

items: combined validation plus holdout 15 bps CAGR > 0
combined validation plus holdout 15 bps maximum positive year contribution <= 0.50
zero duplicate missing nonfinite identity or execution failures

interpretation.dominant_session: Whichever B2 or B3 has the larger zero-cost combined validation-plus-holdout log return; ties are reported as TIE.
